message.content:
Yes, in 2026 the main example is Bittensor (TAO), which uses a Proof-of-Intelligence (PoI) consensus mechanism. Instead of traditional hash-based mining (like Bitcoin), "miners" run machine learning models and compete by providing useful AI outputs—such as training or inference tasks across specialized subnets. The network rewards the most valuable contributions in TAO tokens.⁠Cryptopotato

This is the closest form of cryptocurrency where AI directly participates in "mining" by performing computational intelligence work rather than brute-force hashing.

Other related developments (not true AI mining):
Traditional crypto mining optimized by AI — Tools and platforms use AI for energy optimization, hardware scheduling, profitability switching, and predictive maintenance on Bitcoin, Kaspa, Monero, etc. This is AI assisting human miners, not AI doing the mining itself.⁠Outlookindia
Bitcoin miners pivoting to AI — Many large-scale Bitcoin mining operations are repurposing GPU/ASIC infrastructure and power capacity for AI/HPC workloads (e.g., data centers for model training/inference), sometimes generating up to 70% of revenue from AI by late 2026. This is infrastructure sharing, not AI mining a crypto.⁠Coindesk
Decentralized GPU networks — Projects like Render (RNDR) or io.net let users contribute compute (often GPUs) for AI rendering/training tasks and earn tokens. This rewards providing resources for AI, but the "mining" is still hardware-based contribution rather than autonomous AI performing the work.
Experimental cases — There have been lab incidents (e.g., an Alibaba-linked AI agent autonomously attempting to mine crypto by diverting resources), but these are not production systems or intended network designs.⁠Livescience

No major traditional PoW coins (Bitcoin, etc.) are designed for AI to mine them directly in 2026—Bittensor remains the standout for AI-native participation. Traditional mining remains dominated by ASICs/GPUs with AI only as an efficiency layer.

For practical implementation, check Bittensor subnets if you're interested in running models to earn; most other mining in 2026 focuses on efficiency tools or hardware repurposing rather than pure AI-driven consensus.
